channel.title: SEA Cooperative & Adoption Tracker
channel.description: Ground-level cooperative activity and business practice change across Southeast Asian food systems — smallholder organising, sourcing commitments, and certification uptake.

item.title: Aquaspark rebuilds Asia-Pacific aquaculture due-diligence framework around smallholder producer network depth
item.description: Singapore-based impact investor Aquaspark has rebuilt its Asia-Pacific aquaculture due-diligence framework to centre smallholder producer network depth as a core investment criterion, effective Q3 2026. Eleven existing portfolio companies are undergoing re-assessment under the new framework, and three partner co-investors have indicated they will adopt the framework for parallel deal review. Aquaspark described the change as a structural shift in how the firm defines aquaculture investability, rather than an ESG overlay.

item.title: An Giang Rice Federation signs $18m direct-export contract bypassing traditional exporters
item.description: The An Giang Rice Federation, representing 2,400 smallholder rice-growing households across three Mekong Delta districts, has signed a five-year direct-export contract worth $18 million with two European specialty distributors. The agreement bypasses Vietnam's traditional milling-exporter intermediaries entirely and is underpinned by cooperative-owned drying and storage infrastructure commissioned last year. Federation leaders described the contract as a proof point for smallholder-centred export architecture.

item.title: Nestle SEA lifts regenerative-agriculture sourcing target to 50 per cent of regional volume by 2030
item.description: Nestle's Southeast Asia unit has revised its regenerative-agriculture sourcing target upward, committing to source 50 per cent of regional agricultural volume under regenerative terms by 2030. Dairy in Malaysia, coffee in Vietnam, and coconut in the Philippines are named as priority supply sheds, with third-party verification handled by Bureau Veritas and biennial public reporting.

item.title: Wilmar publishes agroecological sourcing roadmap with 30 per cent share target by 2032
item.description: Wilmar International has published a transition roadmap committing to a 30 per cent agroecological sourcing share across its Southeast Asian upstream supply by 2032. The document uses the term agroecology rather than the firm's prior sustainable-agriculture framing and sets explicit smallholder-equity targets alongside hectare-based metrics. Analysts note the roadmap goes meaningfully further than Wilmar's 2024 NDPE commitments.

item.title: Tonle Sap Fisheries Federation consolidates 23 community cooperatives and secures $6.5m cold-chain facility
item.description: Twenty-three community fisheries cooperatives around Cambodia's Tonle Sap have formed a national federation and simultaneously closed a $6.5 million blended-finance package to build shared cold-chain infrastructure. The federation structure is designed to give smallholder fishers real bargaining power against industrial trawling encroachment, and the financing is structured to keep ownership of the cold-chain assets inside the cooperative system.

item.title: Sumatra Arabica cooperative achieves bird-friendly certification across 2,600 hectares
item.description: A 700-member Arabica coffee cooperative in Aceh has completed bird-friendly certification covering 2,600 hectares of shade-grown plots. The cooperative describes the certification as a practical milestone rather than a goal in itself — part of a longer agroecological programme linking forest cover to member livelihoods.

item.title: Thai organic rice farmer union secures direct-export licence after three-year campaign
item.description: A coalition of organic rice smallholders in Thailand's Yasothon province has been granted a direct export licence following a three-year administrative campaign. Pre-contracted container shipments to specialty distributors in Germany and Singapore are scheduled from May, with proceeds distributed back to member households under a transparent formula.

item.title: Olam signs five-year pricing-floor agreement with 2,300-member Sulawesi cacao cooperative
item.description: Olam Group has signed a five-year pricing-floor agreement with a 2,300-member cacao cooperative in South Sulawesi, guaranteeing a minimum price independent of commodity market movements. The contract commits the cooperative to agroforestry practices, an arrangement intermediate between conventional off-take and fully smallholder-centred direct trade.

item.title: Mindanao banana cooperative publicly rejects plantation-expansion offer citing transition lock-in
item.description: A 1,100-member banana-growing cooperative in Bukidnon has publicly rejected an offer from a multinational fruit exporter to convert 600 hectares of diversified smallholder land into a single-variety plantation. Cooperative leadership cited concerns about transition lock-in and long-term soil degradation, committing instead to continued mixed-crop management.

item.title: Myanmar mungbean cooperative in Sagaing achieves EU traceability compliance across 4,700 plots
item.description: A mungbean cooperative in Myanmar's Sagaing region has completed EU deforestation-regulation traceability compliance covering 4,700 farmer plots, unlocking direct European buyer access. Cooperative leadership cautioned that compliance alone does not substitute for the deeper soil and water programme the cooperative has planned over the following three seasons.

item.title: East Java dairy cooperative overhauls governance to give member-producers board majority
item.description: A 4,100-member dairy cooperative in East Java has completed a governance overhaul giving member-producers a majority on its board, with rotating representation from women dairy farmers. The restructuring is being watched by other regional cooperatives as a possible template for smallholder-equity reforms.

item.title: Battambang rice cooperative commissions solar-powered irrigation network across 480 hectares
item.description: A Cambodian rice cooperative in Battambang has commissioned a solar-powered irrigation network covering 480 hectares of member plots. The network was financed through a mix of member savings and a small concessional loan from a regional rural development fund, keeping ownership and maintenance inside the cooperative structure.

item.title: Southeast Asian cooperative development networks announce regional peer-learning partnership
item.description: Four country-level cooperative development networks across SEA have announced a joint peer-learning partnership, with a twice-yearly exchange programme covering governance reform, cold-chain infrastructure, and direct-export licensing. The partnership is starting modestly but is being read as a move toward regional cooperative-to-cooperative coordination independent of donor agendas.

item.title: Singapore grocery chain doubles plant-based shelf space across 74 stores
item.description: A major Singapore grocery chain has doubled its plant-based protein shelf space across 74 stores, positioning the expansion as part of a climate-smart protein retail strategy. The move is supported by a marketing campaign emphasising taste and convenience, with no stated cooperative or smallholder sourcing component.

item.title: Global plant-based protein market projected to reach $78 billion by 2030
item.description: A widely-circulated global market forecast projects the plant-based protein category will reach $78 billion in annual revenue by 2030, with North American and European markets carrying the majority of growth. The forecast is generic to the category and does not differentiate SEA-specific dynamics, smallholder sourcing, or farming-systems transition.
